primary_meaning: xenesthesia, cross-sensory perception where stimulation of one sense triggers perception in another
secondary_meaning: neurological phenomenon where senses are involuntarily linked or crossed
usage_notes: Use with 'la xénesthésie', 'cas de xénesthésie', 'perception xénesthésique'
common_collocations: xénesthésie synesthétique, perception xénesthésique, cas de xénesthésie
image_search_query: synesthesia cross-sensory perception colored numbers sound
image_context: Visual representation showing how different senses connect in synesthetic perception
example_1: La xénesthésie désigne le phénomène où une stimulation sensorielle provoque une perception dans un autre sens.
example_1_translation: Xenesthesia refers to the phenomenon where sensory stimulation in one modality triggers perception in another.
example_2: Certains individus présentent une xénesthésie remarquable leur permettant de percevoir les couleurs en écoutant de la musique.
example_2_translation: Some individuals have remarkable xenesthesia allowing them to perceive colors while hearing music.
example_3: Les chercheurs étudient les mécanismes neurologiques de la xénesthésie pour comprendre l'intégration sensorielle.
example_3_translation: Researchers study the neurological mechanisms of xenesthesia to understand sensory integration.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'kseh-neh-steh-ZEE'. Stress on 'ZEE'. The 'x' sounds like 'ks'.
memory_hook: From Greek 'xenos' (foreign) + 'aesthesis' (sensation). Foreign sensations crossing between modalities.
related_words: synesthésie, sensation, perception, neurologie, sensibilité sensorielle
common_mistakes: Not confusing 'xénesthésie' with 'synesthésie' - both describe cross-sensory phenomena with nuanced differences.

primary_meaning: yogurt, a fermented milk product made by bacterial cultures
secondary_meaning: cultured dairy food containing live beneficial microorganisms and probiotic bacteria
common_collocations: yogourt nature, yogourt aromatisé, ferments lactiques, culture probiotique
image_search_query: yogurt dairy fermentation probiotic culture bowl
image_context: Bowl of creamy yogurt with fresh fruit and granola toppings
example_1: Le yogourt contient des probiotiques bénéfiques pour l'équilibre de la flore intestinale.
example_1_translation: Yogurt contains beneficial probiotics for intestinal flora balance.
example_2: La fabrication du yogourt nécessite l'utilisation de cultures de ferments lactiques spécifiques.
example_2_translation: Yogurt production requires the use of specific lactic ferment cultures.
example_3: Le yogourt grec offre une teneur protéique plus élevée que le yogourt classique.
example_3_translation: Greek yogurt offers higher protein content than traditional yogurt.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'yoh-GOOR'. Stress on 'GOOR'. The final 't' is not pronounced.
memory_hook: From Turkish 'yoğurt'. Ancient fermented dairy food with Balkan and Middle Eastern origins.
related_words: yaourt, lait, fermentation, probiotique, bactérie lactique, produit laitier
common_mistakes: 'Yogourt' (Quebec/French) vs 'yaourt' (French standard) - both correct spellings in French.

primary_meaning: zodiac, the belt of constellations through which the sun appears to pass annually
secondary_meaning: in astrology, the twelve astrological signs and their associated symbolic meanings
usage_notes: Use with 'le zodiaque', 'signe du zodiaque', 'constellation zodiacale'
common_collocations: signe du zodiaque, constellation zodiacale, roue du zodiaque, astres zodiacaux
image_search_query: zodiac wheel constellations astrology signs symbols
image_context: Traditional zodiac wheel showing all twelve astrological signs in circular arrangement
example_1: Le zodiaque divise le ciel en douze constellations selon l'orbite apparente du soleil.
example_1_translation: The zodiac divides the sky into twelve constellations based on the sun's apparent path.
example_2: Les signes du zodiaque ont chacun des caractéristiques supposées distinctes en astrologie.
example_2_translation: Each zodiac sign has supposedly distinct characteristics in astrology.
example_3: L'étude du zodiaque remonte aux civilisations mésopotamiennes anciennes.
example_3_translation: The study of the zodiac dates back to ancient Mesopotamian civilizations.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'zoh-dee-AHK'. Stress on 'AHK'. The 'z' sounds like English 'z'.
memory_hook: From Greek 'zoidiakos' (of animals). The zodiac depicts animal and mythical figures.
related_words: constellation, astrologie, signe, horoscope, astronomie, mythologie
common_mistakes: Zodiaque refers to both the astronomical belt and astrological system of signs.

primary_meaning: acupuncturist, a practitioner of acupuncture therapy
secondary_meaning: trained professional who inserts fine needles at specific body points for healing
common_collocations: acuponcteur qualifié, acuponcteur traditionnel, acuponcteur licencié, pratique acupuncturale
image_search_query: acupuncturist practitioner needles meridian points healing
image_context: Acupuncturist performing treatment with fine needles on patient's body
example_1: L'acuponcteur expérimenté insère des aiguilles fines à des points précis du corps.
example_1_translation: The experienced acupuncturist inserts fine needles at specific points on the body.
example_2: Consultez un acuponcteur qualifié et licencié pour un traitement sécurisé et efficace.
example_2_translation: Consult a qualified and licensed acupuncturist for safe and effective treatment.
example_3: Les acuponcteurs traditionnels possèdent une connaissance approfondie des méridiens énergétiques.
example_3_translation: Traditional acupuncturists possess deep knowledge of energy meridians.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'ah-koo-pohn-KTÖR'. Stress on 'KTÖR'. French medical practitioner term.
memory_hook: From 'acu-' (needle) + 'puncture' + '-eur' (person). One who punctures with needles.
related_words: acupuncture, aiguille, thérapie, médecine traditionnelle, guérison, méridien
common_mistakes: 'Acuponcteur' is the French term; 'acupuncteur' is less standard but sometimes used.

primary_meaning: stammering or stuttering; halting or faltering speech
secondary_meaning: hesitant utterance characterized by repetition or prolongation of sounds
common_collocations: balbutiement hésitant, balbutiement émotionnel, balbutiement nerveux
image_context: Person showing hesitation and difficulty while speaking
example_1: Le balbutiement du jeune enfant disparaît généralement complètement avec l'âge.
example_1_translation: The stammering of young children generally disappears completely with age.
example_2: Ses balbutiements hésitants trahissaient clairement son émotion et sa nervosité.
example_2_translation: His hesitant stammering clearly betrayed his emotion and nervousness.
example_3: Certains orateurs brillants ont dû surmonter des problèmes de balbutiement dans leur jeunesse.
example_3_translation: Some brilliant orators had to overcome stammering problems in their youth.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'bahl-boo-tee-MAHN'. Stress on final 'MAHN'. Five syllables.
memory_hook: From 'balbutier' (to stammer/falter) + '-ment' (abstract noun). The act of stammering.
related_words: bégaiement, parole, hésitation, nervosité, communication, langage

primary_meaning: coenesthesia, the subjective sense of overall bodily well-being or presence
secondary_meaning: in psychiatry, distorted perception of bodily sensations or body awareness
usage_notes: Use with 'la cénesthésie', 'trouble cénesthésique', 'perturbation de la cénesthésie'
common_collocations: trouble cénesthésique, cénesthésie perturbée, cénesthésie altérée
image_search_query: body awareness sensation consciousness mind-body connection
image_context: Abstract representation of body awareness and conscious perception
example_1: La cénesthésie correspond à la conscience diffuse et globale de son propre corps.
example_1_translation: Coenesthesia corresponds to the diffuse and global awareness of one's own body.
example_2: Les troubles de la cénesthésie peuvent indiquer des pathologies neurologiques ou psychiatriques.
example_2_translation: Coenesthesia disorders may indicate neurological or psychiatric pathologies.
example_3: La perturbation de la cénesthésie est parfois un symptôme chez les patients atteints de dépression.
example_3_translation: Coenesthesia disturbance is sometimes a symptom in depressed patients.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'sen-eh-steh-ZEE'. Stress on final 'ZEE'. Related to 'anesthesia'.
memory_hook: From Greek 'koine' (common) + 'aesthesis' (sensation). Common or general bodily sensation.
related_words: sensation corporelle, perception, conscience, neurologie, psychiatrie
common_mistakes: Not confusing 'cénesthésie' with 'proprioception' - related but distinct concepts.

primary_meaning: dynamic, relating to or characterized by motion, change, or energy
secondary_meaning: in physics, relating to forces that produce motion or affect systems
common_collocations: physique dynamique, système dynamique, analyse dynamique, approche dynamique
image_search_query: physics motion force energy dynamic system diagram
image_context: Complex diagram showing forces, motion, and dynamic systems
example_1: La physique dynamique étudie les forces et leurs effets sur le mouvement des objets.
example_1_translation: Dynamic physics studies forces and their effects on object motion.
example_2: L'économie mondiale suit une tendance économique complexe et constamment dynamique.
example_2_translation: The global economy follows a complex and constantly dynamic economic trend.
example_3: Les systèmes dynamiques non-linéaires présentent des comportements imprévisibles fascinants.
example_3_translation: Non-linear dynamic systems exhibit fascinating unpredictable behaviors.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'dee-nah-MIK'. Stress on 'MIK'. Similar to English 'dynamic'.
memory_hook: From Greek 'dynamis' (power/force). Related to the force that causes change.
related_words: force, mouvement, énergie, physique, système, changement
common_mistakes: 'Dynamique' describes processes; 'dynamo' refers to a device generating power.

primary_meaning: euphoric; tending to produce euphoria or exaggerated sense of well-being
secondary_meaning: relating to substances or conditions that induce elevated mood or pleasure
common_collocations: effet euphorisant, propriétés euphorisantes, substance euphorisante, action euphorisante
image_search_query: euphoria happiness mood elevation brain chemical pleasure
image_context: Brain illustration showing activation of pleasure and reward centers
example_1: Les effets euphorisants de certaines substances sont temporaires mais intensément dangereux.
example_1_translation: The euphoric effects of certain substances are temporary but intensely dangerous.
example_2: La musique possède des propriétés euphorisantes reconnues par la neuroscience.
example_2_translation: Music possesses euphoric properties recognized by neuroscience.
example_3: Les drogues euphorisantes créent une dépendance psychologique rapide et problématique.
example_3_translation: Euphoric drugs create rapid and problematic psychological dependence.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'uh-foh-ree-ZAHN'. Stress on 'ZAHN'. French participle form.
related_words: euphorie, bonheur, drogue, psychopharmacologie, plaisir, dépendance
common_mistakes: 'Euphorisant' describes the effect; 'euphorique' describes the state or person.

primary_meaning: fluoroscopy, real-time X-ray imaging technique for visualizing internal body structures
secondary_meaning: medical imaging modality using fluorescent screen to display continuous X-ray images
common_collocations: examen fluoroscopique, imagerie fluoroscopique, technique fluoroscopique
synonyms: radiographie dynamique, imagerie radiologique en temps réel
image_search_query: fluoroscopy real-time X-ray imaging medical procedure
image_context: Medical technician performing fluoroscopic procedure with X-ray equipment
example_1: La fluoroscopie permet aux médecins de visualiser les mouvements des organes en temps réel.
example_1_translation: Fluoroscopy allows doctors to visualize organ movements in real time.
example_2: L'exposition prolongée aux rayons ionisants lors d'une fluoroscopie présente certains risques biologiques.
example_2_translation: Prolonged exposure to ionizing radiation during fluoroscopy presents certain biological risks.
example_3: La fluoroscopie est une technique d'imagerie essentielle pour les procédures interventionnelles.
example_3_translation: Fluoroscopy is an essential imaging technique for interventional procedures.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'floo-oh-ros-KOH-pee'. Stress on 'KOH'. Greek-derived medical term.
memory_hook: From Latin 'fluere' (to flow) + Greek 'skopein' (to see). To see flowing/dynamic images.
related_words: radiographie, imagerie médicale, rayons X, diagnostic, procédure médicale
common_mistakes: Fluoroscopy is dynamic (real-time); radiography is static (snapshot) imaging.

primary_meaning: galactic, relating to or belonging to a galaxy or the Milky Way
secondary_meaning: of or pertaining to the galaxy as a large-scale stellar system
common_collocations: centre galactique, halo galactique, amas galactique, structure galactique
image_search_query: galaxy Milky Way galactic structure cosmic dust
image_context: Spiral galaxy image showing galactic disk, arms, and central bulge
example_1: La Voie lactée est une galaxie spirale galactique parmi des centaines de milliards.
example_1_translation: The Milky Way is one spiral galactic galaxy among hundreds of billions.
example_2: Les voyages galactiques restent pour le moment du domaine exclusif de la science-fiction.
example_2_translation: Galactic travels remain for now in the exclusive realm of science fiction.
example_3: Les structures galactiques s'étendent sur des millions d'années-lumière.
example_3_translation: Galactic structures extend over millions of light-years.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'gah-lahk-TIK'. Stress on 'TIK'. Similar to English 'galactic'.
memory_hook: From Greek 'gala' (milk). Referring to the Milky Way; milk-colored galaxy.
related_words: galaxie, étoile, univers, cosmologie, espace, constellation

primary_meaning: helicon, a large brass instrument of the tuba family designed for marching bands
secondary_meaning: a circular or spiral configuration of a bass wind instrument allowing easier transport
common_collocations: hélicon circulaire, tuba hélicon, musicien d'hélicon
synonyms: tuba circulaire, instrument à vent grave
image_search_query: helicon musical instrument brass band circular tuba
image_context: Helicon instrument showing circular brass design with keys and bell
example_1: L'hélicon est un instrument à vent en cuivre appartenant à la famille des tubas graves.
example_1_translation: The helicon is a copper wind instrument belonging to the low tuba family.
example_2: L'hélicon circulaire permet une portabilité beaucoup plus facile que le tuba classique.
example_2_translation: The circular helicon allows much easier portability than the classical tuba.
example_3: Les musiciens jouant de l'hélicon produisent des sons graves et profonds dans les fanfares.
example_3_translation: Musicians playing the helicon produce deep and profound sounds in brass bands.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'eh-lee-KON'. Stress on 'KON'. The 'h' is silent.
memory_hook: From Greek 'helix' (spiral). The instrument's spiral/circular shape design.
related_words: tuba, instrument à vent, cuivre, musique, fanfare, orchestre

primary_meaning: immunosuppression or immunodepression; weakening or suppression of immune system function
secondary_meaning: condition of reduced immune response making the body vulnerable to infections
common_collocations: immunodépression sévère, immunodépression relative, patient immunodéprimé
image_search_query: immune system suppression white blood cells weakened
image_context: Microscopic view showing reduced immune cell populations
example_1: L'immunodépression est un effet secondaire fréquent et important de la chimiothérapie.
example_1_translation: Immunosuppression is a frequent and significant side effect of chemotherapy.
example_2: Les patients souffrant d'immunodépression grave doivent éviter tout contact avec des sources d'infection.
example_2_translation: Patients with severe immunosuppression must avoid contact with sources of infection.
example_3: Le VIH provoque progressivement une immunodépression menant au SIDA s'il n'est pas traité.
example_3_translation: HIV gradually causes immunosuppression leading to AIDS if untreated.
related_words: immunité, système immunitaire, infection, maladie, virus, traitement
common_mistakes: 'Immunodépression' is the condition; 'immunodéprimé' is the adjective describing affected person.

primary_meaning: juxtalinear, referring to a translation presented word-by-word alongside the original text
secondary_meaning: displaying original text and translation on adjacent lines or columns for comparison
common_collocations: édition juxtalinéaire, traduction juxtalinéaire, texte juxtalinéaire, disposition juxtalinéaire
synonyms: traduction parallèle, traduction accolée, disposition bilingue
image_search_query: juxtalinear translation parallel text side-by-side comparison
image_context: Page showing original language text next to word-for-word translation
example_1: L'édition juxtalinéaire présente le texte original grec directement à côté de sa traduction française.
example_1_translation: The juxtalinear edition presents the original Greek text directly next to its French translation.
example_2: Les manuscrits juxtalinéaires facilitent grandement l'étude comparative des langues anciennes.
example_2_translation: Juxtalinear manuscripts greatly facilitate comparative study of ancient languages.
example_3: Les étudiants préfèrent souvent les textes juxtalinéaires pour apprendre les langues classiques.
example_3_translation: Students often prefer juxtalinear texts for learning classical languages.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'jook-tah-li-neh-EHR'. Stress on final 'EHR'. French academic term.
related_words: traduction, parallèle, texte, langue, linguistique, étude
common_mistakes: Juxtalinear refers to layout/presentation method, not the quality of translation.

primary_meaning: keratophyte, a plant or organism with a horn-like or corneous structure
secondary_meaning: referring to organisms adapted to environments with hardened or horn-like structures
common_collocations: structure keratophyte, algue keratophyte, plante keratophyte
image_search_query: horn-shaped plant structure algae botanical morphology
image_context: Microscopic view of horn-shaped algal or plant structure
example_1: Le keratophyto est un organisme ayant développé des structures ressemblant à des cornes.
example_1_translation: The keratophyte is an organism that has developed horn-like structures.
example_2: La structure keratophyte de cette algue spéciale lui permet de flotter et survivre.
example_2_translation: The keratophyte structure of this special algae allows it to float and survive.
example_3: Les keratophytes représentent une adaptation morphologique intéressante à l'environnement aquatique.
example_3_translation: Keratophytes represent an interesting morphological adaptation to aquatic environments.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'ker-ah-toh-FEE-toh'. Stress on 'FEE'. Greek-derived botanical term.
memory_hook: From Greek 'keras' (horn) + 'phyton' (plant). A plant with horn-like structures.
related_words: plante, algue, botanique, morphologie, structure, adaptation
common_mistakes: 'Keratophyte' is a rare botanical term; context usually clarifies meaning.

primary_meaning: leukotyping or leukocyte typing; classification and identification of white blood cell types
secondary_meaning: laboratory technique for differentiating leukocyte populations by characteristics
usage_notes: Use with 'un leucotypage', 'examen de leucotypage', 'analyse leucotypique'
common_collocations: leucotypage cellulaire, examen leucotypique, classification leucotypique
synonyms: numération des globules blancs, formule leucocytaire
image_search_query: white blood cell typing leukocyte classification microscopy
image_context: Laboratory microscope showing white blood cell identification and counting
example_1: Le leucotypage permet d'identifier et de classifier les différents types de globules blancs.
example_1_translation: Leukotyping allows identification and classification of different white blood cell types.
example_2: Le leucotypage est une analyse diagnostique essentielle pour le diagnostic des leucémies.
example_2_translation: Leukotyping is an essential diagnostic analysis for leukemia diagnosis.
example_3: L'examen de leucotypage aide les hématologues à comprendre le profil immunitaire du patient.
example_3_translation: Leukotyping examination helps hematologists understand the patient's immune profile.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'luh-koh-tee-PAHZH'. Stress on 'PAHZH'. Medical hematology term.
related_words: globule blanc, hématologie, immunologie, diagnostic, laboratoire, maladie
common_mistakes: 'Leucotypage' is laboratory classification; 'leucocytose' is elevated white cell count.
